candidates.name: datahub-project/datahub

evidence.title: Make Airflow plugin fully compatible with Airflow >=2.7

avoid: High operational complexity without a minimal profile
Tight coupling between ingestion and UI

candidates.name: MarquezProject/marquez

evidence.title: Very slow /api/v1/jobs endpoint after upgrading to 0.50.0

replicate: Lineage service pattern: API + storage + run model
avoid: Using lineage store as system of record for domain data

candidates.name: open-policy-agent/opa
replicate: Separation of policy decisions from application logic
avoid: Non-reproducible decisions due to hidden inputs

candidates.name: kyverno/kyverno

evidence.title: [Bug] Memory leak causes Kyverno Pods to crash with OOMKilled in loaded conditions

replicate: Rule enforcement with explicit test cases
Exception handling as a deliberate workflow

candidates.name: sigstore/cosign

evidence.title: VAULT KMS signing and verification broken after rotating a key in vault

replicate: Treat build outputs as auditable artefacts
avoid: Adopting signing without clear artefact inventory

evidence.title: OpenAPI spec for the REST API

candidates.name: dagster-io/dagster
replicate: Idempotent pipeline steps with run metadata
Run logs and retries as evidence
avoid: Tight coupling to one orchestrator runtime

evidence.title: [Bug] dbtCore - after upgrading, can't run DBT
bug: langfuse docker compose failing on arm linux machines
[BUG] DSPy connector not showing the token count, although it is returned in the request

patterns.name: Policy-as-Code Governance Gates
patterns.summary: Define audit gates as versioned policies with explicit inputs/outputs and test fixtures; keep policy evaluation separate from application code.
tradeoffs: Policy sprawl risk: requires ownership, review, and deprecation policy.
Performance considerations for complex policies; requires profiling and caching.

isa_mapping.change_outline: Introduce a policy evaluation boundary for governance rules (inputs: artefact metadata; outputs: allow/deny + reasons). Start with 1-2 high-value gates (evidence completeness, citation presence) and add tests as fixtures.
isa_mapping.no_console_strategy: Use existing structured logger (`server/utils/server-logger.ts`) for diagnostics; avoid direct output API usage.

patterns.name: Provenance Events (OpenLineage-style)
patterns.summary: Model ingestion and transformation runs as immutable events with stable identifiers, timestamps, inputs/outputs, and run facets; store as auditable artefacts.
tradeoffs: Requires consistent entity IDs; retrofitting IDs later is expensive.
High-cardinality facets can inflate storage and query costs.

isa_mapping.change_outline: Add a minimal provenance event table and emit one event per ingestion step (source fetch, parse, normalise, embed, persist). Link events to existing pipeline execution logs.
isa_mapping.no_console_strategy: Emit events via DB writes and structured logger; avoid direct output API usage.

patterns.name: Schema-Validated Evidence Artefacts in CI
patterns.summary: Treat generated artefacts (scorecards, catalogues, benchmarks) as schema-validated evidence. Enforce parsing + schema validation in CI before merge.
tradeoffs: Schemas require maintenance; breaking schema changes must be versioned.

isa_mapping.change_outline: Define JSON schemas for each evidence artefact and enforce AJV validation in CI. Add drift checks by comparing current vs previous artefact snapshots.
isa_mapping.no_console_strategy: CI steps rely on exit codes and structured artefact output; avoid direct output API usage.

patterns.name: Deterministic Repo Map for Humans and Agents
patterns.summary: Generate and maintain deterministic repo maps (tree + capability index + contracts) so both humans and automation can navigate without guesswork.
tradeoffs: Repo maps must be regenerated consistently; otherwise they become misleading.

isa_mapping.change_outline: Add a machine-readable capability map (JSON) that points to entrypoints, routers, schemas, and tests. Validate it in CI.
isa_mapping.no_console_strategy: Generator writes to files and fails via exit code.

patterns.name: Trace-First LLM Calls + Evaluation Loop
patterns.summary: Treat each LLM call as a traceable unit with inputs, outputs, model config, tool calls, and evaluation results; store datasets and run evaluations continuously.
tradeoffs: Trace data can contain sensitive content; requires retention, redaction, and access controls.
High volume tracing increases storage costs; requires sampling and aggregation.

isa_mapping.change_outline: Add a trace table keyed by request ID + invocation ID; store prompt template version, retrieval set IDs, and evaluation scores. Provide an evaluation runner that uses fixed datasets.
isa_mapping.no_console_strategy: Persist trace rows and use structured server logger; avoid direct output API usage.

patterns.name: Idempotent Ingestion with Run Ledgers
patterns.summary: Make ingestion steps idempotent and record every run with inputs, retries, outcomes, and artefact references; treat the ledger as audit evidence.
tradeoffs: Requires careful definition of idempotency keys and retry semantics.

isa_mapping.change_outline: Standardise on run_id and idempotency keys per pipeline. Persist run metadata and artefact IDs; expose via monitoring router.
isa_mapping.no_console_strategy: Use DB ledger + structured logger only.

patterns.name: Evidence Artefacts that are Independently Verifiable
patterns.summary: Produce evidence artefacts that can be verified by third parties (signatures, attestations, immutable logs). Tie evidence to commit SHA and build inputs.
tradeoffs: Requires defining artefact inventory (datasets, embeddings, mappings) and storage strategy.

isa_mapping.change_outline: Define artefact inventory + checksums. Attach provenance metadata to artefacts and store references in DB.
isa_mapping.no_console_strategy: Write evidence metadata to JSON/DB; runtime logging via structured logger only.

patterns.name: Planning Policy Enforced by CI
patterns.summary: Keep planning artefacts canonical and machine-validated; reject uncontrolled planning sprawl via CI gates.
tradeoffs: Overly strict gates can slow iteration; needs explicit exceptions process.

isa_mapping.change_outline: Extend validators to cover evidence artefacts and enforce allowed path sets.
isa_mapping.no_console_strategy: Use exit codes and structured artefacts; avoid direct output API usage.

patterns.name: Security Hygiene in CI (Static Analysis, SECURITY.md)
patterns.summary: Encode security posture as repo artefacts and CI checks (security policy, static analysis, dependency checks).
tradeoffs: False positives require triage; must be owned.

isa_mapping.change_outline: Enable static analysis and dependency scanning workflows. Add security policy doc.
isa_mapping.no_console_strategy: CI tooling uses exit codes and summaries.

patterns.name: Deterministic Generated Artefacts (Canonicalisation)
patterns.summary: Generate deterministic artefacts (mappings, diffs, benchmarks) with stable ordering and schema validation; store diffs as evidence.
tradeoffs: Canonicalisers must be deterministic across platforms; requires tests.

isa_mapping.change_outline: Add canonicalisers for key outputs and validate canonical form in CI.
isa_mapping.no_console_strategy: Use file outputs and exit codes.

patterns.name: Entity Graph Modelling for Standards/Regulations
patterns.summary: Model standards, regulations, obligations, datapoints, and mappings as typed entities with stable IDs and provenance links.
tradeoffs: Entity graphs require strict schema governance to avoid over-generalisation.

isa_mapping.change_outline: Define canonical IDs and relationship tables for regulations, standards, mappings, and evidence sources. Attach provenance metadata to edges.

patterns.name: Correlation IDs Everywhere (Run ID, Trace ID)
patterns.summary: Use stable run_id/trace_id across ingestion, mapping, and LLM steps; persist into ledgers and evidence artefacts for audit tracing.
tradeoffs: Missing propagation breaks traceability; requires integration tests.

isa_mapping.change_outline: Standardise request/run IDs and persist them in all evidence tables and artefacts. Expose filtering in monitoring router.
isa_mapping.no_console_strategy: Use structured logger and DB writes only.

patterns.name: Continuous Benchmark JSON + Drift Detection
patterns.summary: Maintain deterministic benchmark JSON that can be regenerated in CI and diffed to detect drift; require explicit approval for changes.
tradeoffs: Benchmarks require periodic curation; needs ownership and cadence.

isa_mapping.change_outline: Add CI job to regenerate benchmark JSON and fail on unapproved drift. Document override via ADR.
isa_mapping.no_console_strategy: Validation uses parsing + exit codes; no direct output API usage.

patterns.name: Docs Information Architecture that Scales
patterns.summary: Use a stable docs hierarchy with a canonical index; separate spec, governance, how-to, and research to avoid entropy.
tradeoffs: Requires ongoing pruning and link checks.

isa_mapping.change_outline: Add a canonical docs index and enforce link/structure checks in CI.
isa_mapping.no_console_strategy: Docs checks report via exit codes and summaries.

patterns.name: Capability Boundaries via Plugins/Routers
patterns.summary: Scale features with explicit capability modules (plugins/routers), each with ownership and contracts; enforce boundaries in CI.
tradeoffs: Boundary erosion unless ownership is enforced and reviewed.

isa_mapping.change_outline: Define canonical capability modules with runtime contracts and ownership.

ranked.reason: Governance and audit readiness with lowest operational overhead.
Strong provenance and traceability for complex ingestion.
Scales capability modules but needs strict deterministic tooling.
Independent scaling but high governance and audit surface area.
Harder reproducibility and evidence capture without mature tracing/retention.

action_plan.title: Add provenance event model for ingestion and mapping runs
action_plan.value: End-to-end audit tracing across ingestion/mapping/LLM steps.
acceptance_criteria: A run event record exists for each ingestion step with stable run_id and timestamps.
Events link to stored artefacts (source URLs, hashes).
A query endpoint returns run event timelines.
action_plan.no_console_compliance: Use DB writes and structured server logger; avoid direct output API usage.

action_plan.title: Introduce policy-as-code gates for evidence completeness
action_plan.value: Governance gates become auditable and testable; reduces inconsistent enforcement.
acceptance_criteria: At least 2 governance gates exist with tests and fixture inputs.
CI fails when evidence artefacts violate gate rules.
action_plan.no_console_compliance: Gates run as scripts with exit codes; runtime uses structured logger only.

action_plan.title: Define and enforce JSON schemas for all generated evidence artefacts
action_plan.value: Prevents evidence drift; enables deterministic validation and audit readiness.
acceptance_criteria: Every artefact under docs/evidence/_generated has a schema.
Schema validation runs in CI and fails on mismatch.
action_plan.no_console_compliance: Validation uses exit codes; avoid direct output API usage.

action_plan.title: Add LLM trace tables with retention and redaction policy
action_plan.value: Traceable, reproducible LLM outputs with privacy controls.
acceptance_criteria: LLM traces persist prompt template version, retrieval set IDs, and model config.
A retention job deletes or redacts sensitive fields after configured window.
action_plan.no_console_compliance: Use structured server logger; avoid direct output API usage.

action_plan.title: Create a machine-readable capability map for agent navigation
action_plan.value: Deterministic navigation for agents and reviewers; reduces onboarding time.
acceptance_criteria: A JSON capability map exists and is validated in CI.
Map links each capability to entrypoints, routers, schemas, and tests.
action_plan.no_console_compliance: Generator writes files and fails via exit code only.

action_plan.title: Enable security hygiene workflows (static analysis, dependency checks)
action_plan.value: Improves supply-chain assurance and reduces security debt.
acceptance_criteria: Static analysis workflow runs on PRs.
Security policy file exists and is referenced in README.
action_plan.no_console_compliance: CI tooling uses exit codes and summaries.

action_plan.title: Add drift detection for benchmarks and evidence artefacts
action_plan.value: Detects governance/architecture drift early; makes regressions visible.
acceptance_criteria: CI compares regenerated benchmark JSON to committed version and fails on unapproved drift.
A documented override/approval process exists (ADR).
action_plan.no_console_compliance: Use structured diffs and exit codes.

action_plan.title: Standardise run_id propagation through API context
action_plan.value: End-to-end traceability for requests and background jobs.
acceptance_criteria: All major procedures include run_id/trace_id in ledger rows.
Monitoring endpoints can filter by run_id.
action_plan.no_console_compliance: Use structured logger and DB ledger only.

action_plan.title: Define entity ID conventions for regulations, standards, and mappings
action_plan.value: Enables stable joins and provenance; reduces ambiguous linking.
acceptance_criteria: Every regulation/standard/mapping row has a stable canonical ID.
Provenance includes source URL + retrieval timestamp + hash.
action_plan.no_console_compliance: Migration and ETL scripts use structured logging only.

action_plan.title: Refine docs IA with a canonical index and ownership
action_plan.value: Improves maintainability and reduces doc entropy.
acceptance_criteria: Docs index exists with allowed paths.
Link checks run in CI.
action_plan.no_console_compliance: Docs tooling uses exit codes only.
